assumptions.description: Only one scalar doublet (Phi) and SU(2)_D gauge bosons; no additional fields (fermions, extra scalars)
assumptions.required_solution: Add additional fields to test robustness

source.note: Simplifies dynamics but may limit parameter space and physical realism

assumptions.description: SM-Higgs portal (lambda_HPhi |H|^2 |Phi|^2) and gauge kinetic mixing (epsilon/2 F F') neglected for FOPT dynamics
assumptions.required_solution: Include portal couplings and study their impact on FOPT and GW signal

source.note: FOPT analysis is for dark sector only; portal effects could modify transition. Makes model secluded.

assumptions.description: Scalar potential has only quartic term at tree level: V = -lambda (Phi^dagger Phi)^2, classically scale-invariant
assumptions.required_solution: Consider explicit mass terms or other symmetry-breaking patterns

source.note: Mass scales generated radiatively via CW mechanism. Defines the conformal model class.

assumptions.description: Accidental SO(3) custodial symmetry of scalar potential protects vector DM stability
assumptions.required_solution: Explicitly verify custodial symmetry and its implications

source.note: Explicitly unbroken in model definition. May stabilize vector DM candidates.

assumptions.description: Model does not specify dark matter candidate; literature assumes vector DM from SU(2)_D gauge bosons
assumptions.required_solution: Explicitly state DM candidate and production mechanism

source.note: Literature (2109.11558) discusses vector DM from non-thermal production

assumptions.description: Dark sector is in thermal equilibrium with itself
assumptions.required_solution: Verify thermalization conditions for all parameter space

source.note: Validates use of thermal effective potential

assumptions.description: Dark sector temperature T_D = T_SM (xi = 1), assumed thermal equilibrium with SM bath
assumptions.required_solution: Study impact of xi != 1 on cosmology and GW predictions

source.note: Determines g_star for Hubble rate calculation. Fixed at xi=1 without justification of coupling mechanism.

assumptions.description: Boson loops dominate CW beta function; scalar self-coupling contribution to beta_lambda is subdominant compared to gauge sector
assumptions.required_solution: Include scalar loop corrections to beta_lambda for completeness

source.note: Simplifies CW relation to beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2). Valid for g_D not too small.

assumptions.description: Phase transition occurs at temperatures T << v (strong supercooling regime)
assumptions.required_solution: Validate approximations in supercooled regime; consider 3D EFT methods

source.note: Justifies high-T expansion approximation in pipeline. alpha >> 0.5 in PTA-preferred region.

assumptions.description: SU(2)_D gauge coupling range: g_D in [0.01, 12.56] justified by perturbativity (g_D < 4pi)
assumptions.required_solution: Extend to non-perturbative regime if needed

source.note: Standard perturbativity bound; upper limit not tested in actual scans.

assumptions.description: Vacuum expectation value range: v in [100, 1e6] GeV, justified by FOPT temperature range for PTA signal
assumptions.required_solution: Reconcile with PTA-preferred range v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV

source.note: Original model range differs from PTA-preferred region by orders of magnitude.

assumptions.description: FOPT scan used g_D range [0.5, 3.0], narrower than model range [0.01, 12.56]
assumptions.required_solution: Extend scan to full model range or justify truncation

source.note: Scan range chosen for practical reasons; may miss viable regions at low g_D.

assumptions.description: FOPT scan used v range [0.01, 10] GeV, much lower than original model range [100, 1e6] GeV
assumptions.required_solution: Justify discrepancy with original model motivation; validate low-v regime

source.note: Lower v range was necessary to obtain f_peak in PTA frequency band. Changes mass scale significantly.

assumptions.description: PTArcade used log-uniform priors: log10_g_D in [-0.3010, 0.4771] (g_D ~ [0.5, 3.0]), log10_v_GeV in [-2.0, 1.0] (v ~ [0.01, 10] GeV)
assumptions.required_solution: Test sensitivity to prior choice

source.note: Standard choice for Bayesian parameter inference in PTA analysis.

assumptions.description: High-temperature expansion for thermal effective potential (valid in small field/T regime)
assumptions.required_solution: Implement 3D EFT (dimensional reduction) for supercooled transitions; validate high-T expansion in strong supercooling regime

source.note: May break down in strong supercooling regime (T_* << v) where alpha >> 0.5. Literature (2312.12413) discusses NLO corrections.

assumptions.description: Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction and percolation condition
assumptions.required_solution: Use exact percolation criterion or numerical simulations

source.note: Standard approximation in semi-analytic pipelines; accuracy unclear for strong transitions

assumptions.description: Fixed renormalization scale mu = v (no RG running of couplings)
assumptions.required_solution: Implement RG running and study scale dependence; compare with literature (2210.07075)

source.note: Literature uses RG-improved effective potential. Scale dependence may affect results.

assumptions.description: No Daisy resummation for thermal masses (uses high-T expansion without resumming IR-divergent terms)
assumptions.required_solution: Implement Daisy resummation for thermal masses; validate impact on phase transition

source.note: Daisy resummation is particularly important for strongly supercooled transitions (2312.12413).

assumptions.description: Automatic scalar contribution via Coleman-Weinberg mechanism (lambda determined by beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2))
assumptions.required_solution: Verify CW relation matches literature and backend implementation

source.note: CW mechanism is central to conformal model; implementation verified against backend.

assumptions.description: Bulk flow/dissipative bulk flow (dbf) GW spectrum template assumes specific wall velocity (vw=1.0) and sound speed (cs=1/sqrt(3))
assumptions.required_solution: Study dependence on wall velocity and sound speed; validate template for alpha >> 0.5

source.note: dbf template from 2511.15687 selected as validated for large alpha values

assumptions.description: 200 points (50%) of FOPT scan failed due to eternal inflation, primarily at higher g_D or v values
assumptions.required_solution: Investigate eternal inflation regime; understand physical reason for failures; extend scan methodology

source.note: Large failure rate suggests significant parameter space is inaccessible or physically invalid.

assumptions.description: Model produces very large alpha values (up to 10^16) in PTA-preferred region due to Coleman-Weinberg mechanism
assumptions.required_solution: Validate all approximations (high-T expansion, Daisy resummation, template) in alpha >> 0.5 regime

source.note: Strong supercooling regime; many standard approximations may break down. Literature (2312.12413) discusses EFT challenges.

assumptions.description: Discrepancy with literature (2109.11558): backend gives T_p ~ 0.1 v while literature reports sub-GeV T_* for v ~ 100 GeV
assumptions.required_solution: Investigate origin of discrepancy; verify backend implementation; reconcile conventions

source.note: May be due to different conventions, model details, or backend approximations.

assumptions.description: All spectra peak at the lowest PTA frequency bin (-7.53e-09 Hz), suggesting actual peak is below PTA frequency range
assumptions.required_solution: Extend to lower frequencies or verify if spectrum can match within PTA range

source.note: Model may inherently produce GW signals at frequencies below PTA sensitivity.

assumptions.description: PTA-preferred region has v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, much lower than original model range [100, 1e6] GeV, changing all particle masses by 2-3 orders of magnitude
assumptions.required_solution: Re-evaluate model motivation and physical interpretation at low v; check consistency with original literature

source.note: Dark gauge bosons have masses 0.28-0.64 GeV in PTA-preferred region, making them accessible to beam-dump and collider constraints.

assumptions.description: No SM portal couplings included; dark sector is secluded, limiting direct production constraints and cosmological connections
assumptions.required_solution: Study impact of adding portal couplings; assess naturalness of secluded scenario

source.note: Limits applicability of collider and beam dump constraints; affects DM production mechanisms.

assumptions.description: All perturbative calculations assume g_D < 4pi ~ 12.56; validity breaks down at stronger couplings
assumptions.required_solution: Monitor perturbativity throughout parameter space; flag non-perturbative regions

source.note: Standard perturbativity bound; scan ranges respect this.

assumptions.description: High-temperature expansion for thermal effective potential valid when T^2 >> m^2 for all particles; may break down in supercooled regime (T_* << v)
assumptions.required_solution: Implement 3D EFT methods or full thermal potential without high-T expansion; validate in supercooled regime

source.note: Literature (2312.12413, 2210.07075) discusses challenges of EFT description for strongly supercooled transitions.

assumptions.description: dbf GW spectrum template validated for alpha >> 0.5 (strongly supercooled transitions); higgsless template only valid for alpha <= 0.5
assumptions.required_solution: Continue using dbf template; validate against simulations for extreme alpha values (up to 10^16)

source.note: Template choice appropriate for this model's parameter space.

assumptions.description: All constraints requiring portal couplings (beam dump, collider, fixed-target) are not applicable due to epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0 assumption
assumptions.required_solution: Document this limitation clearly; consider adding portal couplings for completeness

source.note: Major limitation: most direct detection constraints cannot be applied to secluded sector.

assumptions.description: Particle_content lists gauge boson A with dof=3 without clarifying these are field components, not degrees of freedom
assumptions.required_solution: Clarify in documentation (already addressed in description)

source.note: Minor ambiguity; actual dof correctly handled in field_dependent_masses

assumptions.description: Field G is named 'Goldstone boson' but has m^2 = lambda chi^2 (massive); physically a pseudo-Goldstone scalar from global symmetry breaking
assumptions.required_solution: Rename to pseudo-Goldstone or massive scalar (already addressed in model.md)

source.note: Misleading naming; corrected in model documentation

assumptions.description: W thermal mass formula assumes same thermal correction for all dof, but transverse modes may have different thermal masses than longitudinal
assumptions.required_solution: Investigate mode-dependent thermal masses; validate impact

source.note: Approximation consistent with pipeline treatment and literature (2109.11558, 2210.07075)

assumptions.description: Scalar loop contributions to beta_lambda omitted in CW relation; approximation valid for g_D not too small
assumptions.required_solution: Include scalar loops for completeness; quantify impact

source.note: Verified in literature (2109.11558, 2210.07075) as valid approximation

assumptions.description: Light scalars (m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV) in PTA-preferred region may affect BBN if they decay during or after BBN
assumptions.required_solution: Compute scalar decay widths and lifetimes; assess BBN impact

source.note: Requires new backend for scalar decay calculations

assumptions.description: Dark sector particles may contribute to N_eff if reheated above decoupling temperature; thermal history not computed
assumptions.required_solution: Compute dark sector thermal history and decoupling temperature

source.note: Requires new backend for thermal history computation

assumptions.description: Vector DM self-interaction cross section ~ g_D^4 / m_W^2 ~ 10^{-2} to 1 cm^2/g in PTA region may violate galactic/cluster constraints
assumptions.required_solution: Compute dark sector scattering amplitudes; compare with observations

source.note: Requires new backend for dark sector scattering calculations

assumptions.description: No RG running of couplings; fixed renormalization scale mu = v may introduce scale dependence in results
assumptions.required_solution: Implement RG running and study scale dependence; compare with literature (2210.07075)

source.note: Literature uses RG-improved effective potential

assumptions.description: Strong supercooling regime (alpha >> 0.5, up to 10^16) may affect applicability of multiple approximations (high-T expansion, Daisy resummation, etc.)
assumptions.required_solution: Systematic validation of all approximations in supercooled regime; consider alternative methods (3D EFT)

source.note: Core theoretical uncertainty for this model

assumptions.description: PTA-preferred region produces sub-GeV dark gauge bosons (m_W ~ 0.28-0.64 GeV) and light scalars (m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV)
assumptions.required_solution: Re-evaluate all constraints for sub-GeV mass range; consider new experimental searches

source.note: Makes particles accessible to beam-dump and collider constraints if portal couplings exist

assumptions.description: dbf template extrapolated to extreme alpha values (up to 10^16); validation may not extend to this regime
assumptions.required_solution: Validate template against simulations at extreme alpha; consider alternative templates

source.note: Template from 2511.15687 validated for large alpha but not necessarily for alpha ~ 10^16

assumptions.description: 50% failure rate due to eternal inflation in FOPT scan; may indicate incomplete parameter space coverage or numerical issues
assumptions.required_solution: Investigate eternal inflation criterion; improve numerical stability; understand physical reasons

source.note: Primarily at higher g_D or v values

required_followups.description: Validate high-temperature expansion and all approximations in strong supercooling regime (alpha >> 0.5)
required_followups.suggested_action: Implement 3D EFT (dimensional reduction) methods; compare with literature (2312.12413, 2210.07075)

required_followups.description: Add SM portal couplings (kinetic mixing, Higgs portal) and study their impact on FOPT and constraints
required_followups.suggested_action: Extend model with epsilon and lambda_HPhi parameters; re-run FOPT and constraint analysis

required_followups.description: Reconcile FOPT scan range with original model motivation; validate low-v regime
required_followups.suggested_action: Run additional scans with v in [100, 1e6] GeV; investigate why PTA prefers low v

required_followups.description: Compute scalar decay widths and lifetimes for BBN analysis
required_followups.suggested_action: Develop backend for scalar decay calculations; assess BBN impact in PTA-preferred region

required_followups.description: Compute dark sector scattering amplitudes for self-interacting DM constraints
required_followups.suggested_action: Develop backend for dark sector scattering; compare with galactic and cluster observations

required_followups.description: Compute dark sector thermal history for N_eff and decoupling analysis
required_followups.suggested_action: Develop backend for thermal history computation; compute decoupling temperature from SM

required_followups.description: Implement RG running of couplings and study scale dependence
required_followups.suggested_action: Add RG running to backend; compare with fixed-scale results; quantify scale uncertainty
